data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-8fa7ecf6">
    <title>try: preflight execution resources before claim and worktree setup</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
ddx try can claim/checkpoint and then fail during worktree setup when the temp worktree or evidence root is out of bytes/inodes or unwritable. In the May 6 incident, git worktree add failed with No space left on device after creating partial execution artifacts, and the failure was treated as ordinary execution_failed instead of a resource guardrail.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:686-714 creates the temp worktree parent, commits/checkpoints, resolves base, and calls WorktreeAdd before any resource-health check of $TMPDIR/ddx-exec-wt or .ddx/executions/.ddx/runs.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:713-714 returns immediately on WorktreeAdd failure, so partial unregistered directories can remain outside the deferred WorktreeRemove path.
- cli/cmd/try.go:306-342 routes ddx try through ExecuteBeadWorker without a pre-claim resource gate that can fail before claiming the target bead.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md:454-467 requires ddx try to validate roots, run one cleanup pass, re-check, and return resource_exhausted without claiming when still unhealthy.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a resource checker abstraction that validates temp worktree root and durable evidence root writability, free bytes, and free inodes where supported.
- Run resource preflight before claim/worktree creation in ddx try paths, using the cleanup manager once before final failure.
- Classify persistent resource failure as resource_exhausted with structured detail, not generic execution_failed/quota.
- Ensure WorktreeAdd failure removes partial unregistered worktree directories created for the attempt.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not decide ddx work loop stop behavior here; sibling ddx work bead consumes the resource_exhausted outcome.
- Do not add background cleanup cadence here.
- Do not implement a real low-disk integration test that fills a filesystem; use injectable resource checker/fake filesystem boundaries.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestTryResourcePreflight_FailsBeforeClaim verifies a failing injected resource checker runs cleanup once, returns resource_exhausted, and leaves the bead open/unclaimed.
2. TestTryResourcePreflight_RechecksAfterCleanup verifies ddx try proceeds when cleanup restores healthy resource status.
3. TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure_RemovesPartialDir verifies a simulated WorktreeAdd ENOSPC failure removes the attempt partial directory under DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR.
4. TestTryResourcePreflight_ChecksEvidenceAndTempRoots verifies both the temp worktree root and durable evidence root are checked for writability and resource health.
5. Production ddx try/work paths call the resource preflight before claim/worktree creation; tests assert the new code path is wired, not just unit-tested.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run "TestTryResourcePreflight|TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:cli, kind:bug, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, resource-exhaustion</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-f3dfde0d" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="05813493f8d513ec26f04a28e32c769dcdcf32bd" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T165037-eedd8843"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `f90f73b67` (`fix: preflight execution resources before worktree setup [ddx-8fa7ecf6]`).

What changed:
- Added worker-side resource preflight to `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` before any worktree setup or git checkpointing, with an injectable checker fallback to the default checker.
- Threaded the checker through the production `ddx try` and `ddx agent execute-bead` paths so the same preflight path is used end to end.
- Added a focused worker-path test proving the preflight fails before any git/worktree operations.
- Kept the existing CLI resource-preflight tests for `try` and `work` as the command-level wiring coverage.

Key references:
- [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go#L131-L140)
- [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go#L678-L710)
- [cli/cmd/try.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/cmd/try.go#L208-L255)
- [cli/cmd/try.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/cmd/try.go#L324-L332)
- [cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go#L1814-L1820)
- [cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_resource_preflight_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_resource_preflight_test.go#L80-L100)
- [cli/cmd/resource_preflight_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-8fa7ecf6-20260506T165037-eedd8843/cli/cmd/resource_preflight_test.go#L45-L84)

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestTryResourcePreflight|TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure' -count=1`
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... -run 'TestTryResourcePreflight|TestExecuteBeadWorktreeAddFailure|TestExecuteBeadResourcePreflight' -count=1`
- `CGO_ENABLED=1 lefthook run pre-commit`

One note: the repository’s pre-commit `go-test` hook requires `CGO_ENABLED=1` because it runs race-enabled tests.
